lostDestinations.source: flyrst.com history; search results confirm TWA served STL until merger 2001
Search results: Imperial Airways helicopter RST-MSP canceled Dec 4 1970
AA began ORD service 1988; historically Ozark/North Central served Chicago pre-deregulation
Search results: RST had KC/Tulsa/Chicago service; Braniff left ~1968

note: RST currently served by AA (DFW) and Delta (ATL,MSP,DTW); lost STL service when TWA merged into AA 2001; Ozark served STL until TWA acquisition ~1986

lostDestinations.source: Search results: Mesaba served TVF as Northwest Airlink from 1984; Delta Connection ended MSP service ~2012 when Boutique Air took over EAS

note: TVF currently EAS-subsidized; Boutique Air operates 8-seat aircraft to MSP; lost mainline jet Delta Connection service ~2012

lostDestinations.source: Search: North Central started service 1952; Mesaba/NW Airlink served BJI; Delta Connection operated with SkyWest after 2012 EAS transition
Wikipedia: Bemidji Airlines operated scheduled service 1981-1991 from Bemidji, Thief River Falls, Worthington, Mankato to MSP

note: BJI is EAS-dependent; currently Delta Connection via SkyWest to MSP; lost multi-stop intrastate service when Bemidji Airlines ceased 1991

lostDestinations.source: Search: BRD currently served by SkyWest as Delta Connection to MSP; EAS-subsidized

note: BRD is EAS-dependent; North Central then Republic then Mesaba/NW Airlink served the route; now SkyWest/Delta Connection; retains MSP service but under EAS subsidy

lostDestinations.source: Search: INL currently EAS-subsidized; North Central/Republic/Northwest Airlink historical carriers; Delta Connection via SkyWest current

note: INL is EAS-dependent; North Central Airlines began service ~1953; currently SkyWest/Delta Connection to MSP under EAS; retains MSP service

lostDestinations.source: Search: HIB EAS-subsidized; Wisconsin Central began service Dec 1946; Northwest Airlink Express I then Mesaba served HIB; now SkyWest/Delta Connection
Search: six routes including Hibbing flown by Express I as Northwest Airlink; Northwest maintenance base opened Duluth 1996 suggesting regional reconfiguration mid-1990s

note: HIB currently EAS-dependent via SkyWest/Delta Connection to MSP; Northwest Airlink crash Dec 1993 killed all 18 on board near HIB

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia St. Cloud Regional Airport: Mesaba Delta Connection ended MSP service Dec 31 2009
Search: SkyWest operated STC-ORD as United Express May 2014 to April 2015, suspended due to poor performance

detail: St. Cloud lost its MSP hub feed (Mesaba/Delta Connection) on Dec 31 2009 and a short-lived ORD United Express service failed in 2015; today only Allegiant seasonal leisure routes remain.
source: Wikipedia St. Cloud Regional Airport; search results

lostDestinations.source: AirTAP/search: North Central 1969-1985, then Mesaba 1981-1986, then Bemidji Aviation EAS until 1992

detail: Worthington lost scheduled air service in 1992 when EAS subsidies ended; North Central Airlines had served MSP since 1969, followed by Mesaba then Bemidji Aviation under the EAS program.
source: AirTAP Worthington Airport Story; Mesaba history FundingUniverse

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Bemidji Airlines: operated scheduled service 1981-1991 from Mankato to MSP; North Central served Mankato until 1969

detail: Mankato lost all scheduled service in 1991 when Bemidji Airlines ceased EAS operations; North Central Airlines last served the city in 1969 and helicopter service tried 1969-1970 also failed.
source: Wikipedia Bemidji Airlines; search results North Central/Mankato history

lostDestinations.source: Search: Mesaba initiated GPZ-MSP service Feb 1973; discontinued March 2004 when Northwest abandoned the route

detail: Grand Rapids (GPZ) was the birthplace of Mesaba Airlines (1944/1973 scheduled origin); Mesaba served the MSP route for 31 years until Northwest Airlines dropped it in March 2004.

lastCarrier: Mississippi Valley Airlines (merged into Air Wisconsin)

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Winona Municipal Airport: Wisconsin Central/North Central 1951-1970, Mississippi Valley Airlines DHC-6 1970s-1985; MVA merged into Air Wisconsin May 17 1985

detail: Winona lost scheduled service when Mississippi Valley Airlines merged into Air Wisconsin on May 17, 1985; North Central Airlines had served the city since 1951.
source: Wikipedia Winona Municipal Airport; Mississippi Valley Airlines Wikipedia

lostDestinations.source: Search: Mississippi Valley Airlines scheduled MSP Dec 1972; Lake State Airways mid-1970s to early 1980s Piper PA-31 Marshall-New Ulm-MSP route

detail: Marshall lost scheduled service in the early 1980s when Lake State Airways ceased operations; prior to that Mississippi Valley Airlines briefly served the MSP route from December 1972.
source: Search results Southwest Minnesota Regional Airport history

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Fergus Falls Municipal Airport: formerly served by Northwest Airlink and United Express; no current scheduled service

detail: Fergus Falls lost all scheduled airline service in the 1990s; Northwest Airlink (Mesaba) and briefly United Express both served the airport before withdrawing, leaving it general aviation only.
source: Wikipedia Fergus Falls Municipal Airport; search results

lostDestinations.source: MPR News Feb 13 2020; American Airlines ending Duluth-Chicago ORD flights April 2020 after launching May 2019
Search: Mesaba inaugurated Duluth-Detroit nonstop Oct 1995 with Avro RJ85; service still active Nov 2001; ended ~2004-2005 as Northwest consolidated

detail: Duluth lost American Airlines Chicago O'Hare service in April 2020 after only 11 months; also lost nonstop Detroit service (Mesaba/Northwest Airlink Avro RJ85 from 1995) in the mid-2000s; United Express now dominant with 5 daily ORD flights as of 2026.
source: MPR News 2020; Mesaba history search results; Northern News Now 2026
